summary: Live-verified award-detail endpoint that returns one award profile object for a known award identifier, combining shared award summary fields with category-specific procurement or financial-assistance sections. This bundle normalizes the cross-endpoint generated award hash as canonical_award_lookup_id: search emits generated_internal_id, this route accepts it as path award_id, and the response echoes generated_unique_award_id.
businessPurpose: Use this endpoint when you already know a specific USAspending award identifier and need the detail-page payload: recipient and agency context, award type and identifiers, award-level financial summary fields, and category-specific contract or assistance metadata. It is the primary detail step after capturing canonical_award_lookup_id from award search or before passing the same identifier into award funding.
analyticalGrain: Exactly one response object per requested award, enriched with nested recipient, agency, accounting-total, and category-specific detail sections rather than a list of transactions or funding rows.

primaryEntities.name: Award
primaryEntities.description: The endpoint is scoped to one requested award_id and returns one award profile object for that award.

primaryEntities.name: Recipient
primaryEntities.description: Each response includes a recipient object with recipient identifiers, parent-recipient context, business categories, and recipient location.

primaryEntities.name: Awarding and funding agencies
primaryEntities.description: Responses include awarding_agency and usually funding_agency objects that identify the top-tier and subtier agencies attached to the award.

measures.name: Award-level obligation and subaward totals
measures.description: Common measures include total_obligation, subaward_count, and total_subaward_amount.

measures.name: Accounting totals by DEFC and outlay measure
measures.description: The endpoint exposes total_account_outlay, total_account_obligation, account_outlays_by_defc, account_obligations_by_defc, and a separate total_outlay measure assembled from financial-account data.

measures.name: Category-specific assistance or loan amounts
measures.description: Assistance and loan branches add fields such as transaction_obligated_amount, total_funding, non_federal_funding, total_loan_value, or total_subsidy_cost depending on award category.

dimensions.name: Award category and type
dimensions.description: category and type determine whether the award is a contract, IDV, grant, direct payment, loan, or other assistance subtype and which nested sections apply.

dimensions.name: Award identifiers
dimensions.description: The response carries identifiers such as generated_unique_award_id and category-specific PIID, FAIN, or URI fields.

dimensions.name: Canonical award lookup id
dimensions.description: Normalize the cross-endpoint handoff identifier as canonical_award_lookup_id: if you start from v2__search__spending_by_award use generated_internal_id as this route's award_id, and treat response.generated_unique_award_id as the same business key for downstream funding or other award drilldowns.

dimensions.name: Procurement classification
dimensions.description: Procurement awards expose contract-detail, PSC, NAICS, and parent-award sections for classification and contract-history interpretation.

dimensions.name: Assistance program context
dimensions.description: Assistance awards expose CFDA program details and a funding_opportunity object.

dimensions.name: Recipient and location context
dimensions.description: Recipient and place_of_performance objects provide organizational and geographic context for the award.

suitableQuestions.name: What does this known award represent and who received it?
suitableQuestions.description: Use the endpoint to retrieve the award summary, recipient details, agencies, and descriptive identifiers for one known award, especially when you already have canonical_award_lookup_id from award search.

suitableQuestions.name: Is this award a procurement award or an assistance award, and which branch-specific details are available?
suitableQuestions.description: category and the presence of procurement versus assistance nested sections make the endpoint suitable for branch-aware parsing.

suitableQuestions.name: What award-level obligation, subaward, DEFC, and outlay metrics are attached to this award?
suitableQuestions.description: The endpoint returns common obligation and subaward totals plus account-based obligation/outlay summaries.

notSuitableFor.name: Searching or filtering across many awards
notSuitableFor.description: This route requires one known award id in the path and returns one award object, not a search result list.

notSuitableFor.name: Transaction-level or funding-row analysis
notSuitableFor.description: The endpoint summarizes one award; it is not the transaction list or award-funding row surface.

notSuitableFor.name: Blindly joining from the display Award ID in search results
notSuitableFor.description: Current-profile and reviewer-backed evidence say the human-facing Award ID may not be the safest follow-up identifier; normalize generated helper ids as canonical_award_lookup_id instead.

joins.description: Normalize these related labels as the semantic alias canonical_award_lookup_id. In v2__search__spending_by_award the alias value is generated_internal_id; pass the same string unchanged as the path award_id on this endpoint rather than using the display Award ID.

joins.to: Award funding or other award drilldown endpoints
joins.description: Treat response.generated_unique_award_id as this endpoint's label for canonical_award_lookup_id and reuse it unchanged in downstream award workflows. In v2__awards__funding the same business key is sent under request body field award_id.

workflows.name: Resolve canonical_award_lookup_id to a full award profile
workflows.description: Start from one normalized award handoff identifier, retrieve the award detail object, then reuse the echoed canonical id in downstream award workflows.

steps.action: Obtain canonical_award_lookup_id. If you are chaining from v2__search__spending_by_award, use generated_internal_id rather than the display Award ID.
Call GET /api/v2/awards/{award_id}/ using canonical_award_lookup_id as the path award_id and keep the canonical trailing-slash path.

Inspect category first, then parse procurement-only sections (latest_transaction_contract_data, parent_award, NAICS/PSC hierarchies) or assistance-only sections (cfda_info, funding_opportunity, assistance financial totals).
When continuing into award funding or related award drilldowns, reuse response.generated_unique_award_id as canonical_award_lookup_id even though downstream request fields may be named award_id.

caveats.statement: Related award-search, award-detail, and award-funding endpoints relabel the same generated award business key. Normalize it as canonical_award_lookup_id: search emits generated_internal_id, this route accepts award_id, and the response exposes generated_unique_award_id.
caveats.impact: Carry the generated identifier unchanged across endpoints and do not substitute the human-facing display Award ID when a generated helper id is available.

caveats.statement: The endpoint accepts more than one identifier form: source code distinguishes numeric internal ids from string award ids and retries generated_unique_award_id_legacy when the string lookup misses.
caveats.impact: Prefer generated current ids when available, but be aware that numeric or legacy ids may also resolve.

caveats.statement: category materially changes the shape of nested data returned by the endpoint.
caveats.impact: Always branch parsing logic before assuming procurement-only or assistance-only objects exist.

caveats.statement: The docs are inconsistent about funding_opportunity naming and total_outlay presence.
caveats.impact: Treat the live/source payload shape as authoritative for those fields and do not confuse total_outlay with total_account_outlay.

caveats.statement: Current-profile evidence indicates error bodies are not uniformly JSON for all invalid string paths.
caveats.impact: Inspect HTTP status and content type before parsing error responses.
